content:
# Joshua Chapter 15 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 238-240 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_015_page_001.jpg`

1. And the lot for the tribe of the sons of Judah, according to their clans, was to the border of Edom, the wilderness of Zin southward, at the extreme south.

2. And their southern border was from the end of the Salt Sea, from the tongue that faces southward.

3. And it went out southward of the Ascent of Akrabbim, and passed along to Zin, and went up on the south side of Kadesh-barnea, and passed along to Hezron, and went up to Addar, and turned about to Karka.

4. And it passed along to Azmon, and went out at the Wadi of Egypt, and the border's goings out were at the sea. This shall be your southern border.

5. And the eastern border was the Salt Sea as far as the mouth of the Jordan. And the border on the northern side was from the tongue of the sea, from the mouth of the Jordan.

6. And the border went up to Beth-hoglah, and passed along north of Beth-arabah, and the border went up to the Stone of Bohan the son of Reuben.

7. And the border went up to Debir from the Valley of Achor, and northward, turning toward Gilgal, which is opposite the Ascent of Adummim, which is south of the wadi. And the border passed along to the waters of En-shemesh, and its goings out were at En-rogel.

8. And the border went up by the Valley of Ben-hinnom to the southern slope of the Jebusite — that is, Jerusalem. And the border went up to the top of the mountain that is before the Valley of Hinnom on the west, which is at the northern end of the Valley of Rephaim.

9. And the border extended from the top of the mountain to the spring of the waters of Nephtoah, and went out to the cities of Mount Ephron. And the border extended to Baalah — that is, Kiriath-jearim.

10. And the border turned about from Baalah westward to Mount Seir, and passed along to the slope of Mount Jearim on the north — that is, Chesalon — and went down to Beth-shemesh, and passed along to Timnah.

11. And the border went out to the slope of Ekron northward, and the border extended to Shikkeron, and passed along to Mount Baalah, and went out to Jabneel. And the goings out of the border were at the sea.

12. And the western border was the Great Sea and its coastline. This is the border surrounding the sons of Judah according to their clans.

13. And to Caleb the son of Jephunneh he gave a portion in the midst of the sons of Judah, according to the command of YHWH to Joshua: Kiriath-arba — the father of Anak — that is, Hebron.

14. And Caleb drove out from there the three sons of Anak: Sheshai, and Ahiman, and Talmai, the descendants of Anak.

15. And he went up from there against the inhabitants of Debir. Now the name of Debir formerly was Kiriath-sepher.

16. And Caleb said, "Whoever strikes Kiriath-sepher and captures it, to him I will give Achsah my daughter as a wife."

17. And Othniel the son of Kenaz, the brother of Caleb, captured it; and he gave him Achsah his daughter as a wife.

18. And it was, when she came to him, that she urged him to ask her father for a field. And she dismounted from the donkey, and Caleb said to her, "What do you want?"

19. And she said, "Give me a blessing. Since you have given me the land of the Negev, give me also springs of water." And he gave her the upper springs and the lower springs.

20. This is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Judah according to their clans.

21. And the cities at the extreme south of the tribe of the sons of Judah, toward the border of Edom in the Negev, were: Kabzeel, and Eder, and Jagur,

22. and Kinah, and Dimonah, and Adadah,

23. and Kedesh, and Hazor, and Ithnan,

24. Ziph, and Telem, and Bealoth,

25. and Hazor-hadattah, and Kerioth-hezron — that is, Hazor —

26. Amam, and Shema, and Moladah,

27. and Hazar-gaddah, and Heshmon, and Beth-pelet,

28. and Hazar-shual, and Beer-sheba, and Biziothiah,

29. Baalah, and Iim, and Ezem,

30. and Eltolad, and Chesil, and Hormah,

31. and Ziklag, and Madmannah, and Sansannah,

32. and Lebaoth, and Shilhim, and Ain, and Rimmon — all the cities were twenty-nine, with their villages.

33. In the Shephelah: Eshtaol, and Zorah, and Ashnah,

34. and Zanoah, and En-gannim, Tappuah, and Enam,

35. Jarmuth, and Adullam, Socoh, and Azekah,

36. and Shaaraim, and Adithaim, and Gederah, and Gederothaim — fourteen cities with their villages.

37. Zenan, and Hadashah, and Migdal-gad,

38. and Dilean, and Mizpeh, and Joktheel,

39. Lachish, and Bozkath, and Eglon,

40. and Cabbon, and Lahmas, and Chithlish,

41. and Gederoth, Beth-dagon, and Naamah, and Makkedah — sixteen cities with their villages.

42. Libnah, and Ether, and Ashan,

43. and Iphtah, and Ashnah, and Nezib,

44. and Keilah, and Achzib, and Mareshah — nine cities with their villages.

45. Ekron, with its towns and its villages.

46. From Ekron to the sea, all that were beside Ashdod, with their villages.

47. Ashdod, its towns and its villages; Gaza, its towns and its villages; as far as the Wadi of Egypt, and the Great Sea with its coastline.

48. And in the hill country: Shamir, and Jattir, and Socoh,

49. and Dannah, and Kiriath-sannah — that is, Debir —

50. and Anab, and Eshtemoh, and Anim,

51. and Goshen, and Holon, and Giloh — eleven cities with their villages.

52. Arab, and Dumah, and Eshan,

53. and Janim, and Beth-tappuah, and Aphekah,

54. and Humtah, and Kiriath-arba — that is, Hebron — and Zior — nine cities with their villages.

55. Maon, Carmel, and Ziph, and Juttah,

56. and Jezreel, and Jokdeam, and Zanoah,

57. Kain, Gibeah, and Timnah — ten cities with their villages.

58. Halhul, Beth-zur, and Gedor,

59. and Maarath, and Beth-anoth, and Eltekon — six cities with their villages.

60. Kiriath-baal — that is, Kiriath-jearim — and Rabbah — two cities with their villages.

61. In the wilderness: Beth-arabah, Middin, and Secacah,

62. and Nibshan, and the City of Salt, and En-gedi — six cities with their villages.

63. But the Jebusites, the inhabitants of Jerusalem — the sons of Judah were not able to drive them out. And the Jebusites have dwelt with the sons of Judah in Jerusalem to this day.

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

1. **YHWH (יהוה):** Rendered as "YHWH" in v. 13, preserving the Tetragrammaton per LOGOS convention rather than substituting "the LORD."

2. **"Sons of Judah" (בְּנֵי יְהוּדָה):** Rendered literally throughout (vv. 1, 12, 13, 20, 21, 63) per LOGOS convention rather than the interpretive "Judahites" or "people of Judah."

3. **"Sons of Anak" (יְלִידֵי הָעֲנָק / בְּנֵי עֲנָק):** In v. 14, the Hebrew *yelide ha-Anak* literally means "those born of Anak" or "descendants of Anak." The term *Anak* functions both as a personal name and as a clan designation for the giant inhabitants of Hebron (cf. Num 13:33).

4. **"Kiriath-arba, the father of Anak" (קִרְיַת אַרְבַּע אֲבִי הָעֲנָק):** V. 13. The Hebrew identifies Arba as "the father of Anak." This preserves the Masoretic syntax. Some translations render "the greatest man among the Anakim," but the formal reading follows the Hebrew construction directly.

5. **"the tongue" (הַלָּשׁוֹן):** Vv. 2, 5. A geographical metaphor for a bay or inlet of the Salt Sea (Dead Sea), rendered literally to preserve the Hebrew image.

6. **"goings out" (תּוֹצְאֹת / מוֹצָא):** The standard Hebrew boundary formula for where a border terminates. Rendered literally rather than paraphrasing as "ended at" to maintain formal equivalence with the recurring geographical formulae of this chapter.

7. **"Valley of Ben-hinnom" (גֵּי בֶן־הִנֹּם):** V. 8. Rendered literally as "Valley of Ben-hinnom" (son of Hinnom) rather than the later Greco-Latin form "Gehenna." This preserves the original Hebrew place-name and its patronymic construction.

8. **"Negev" (נֶגֶב):** Retained as a proper noun in vv. 19, 21 where it designates the specific southern desert region. In vv. 1-4, the directional sense "southward / south" is used where the Hebrew indicates compass direction rather than the region by name.

9. **City counts:** The city tallies given in the Hebrew text (e.g., "twenty-nine" in v. 32, "fourteen" in v. 36) occasionally do not match the number of cities enumerated in the preceding list. This is a well-known text-critical feature of Joshua 15. The LOGOS translation preserves the Masoretic numbers without emendation, following the Leningrad Codex as received.

10. **"to this day" (עַד הַיּוֹם הַזֶּה):** V. 63. A formulaic expression indicating the situation persisted up to the time of the text's composition or final editing, prior to David's capture of Jerusalem (2 Sam 5:6-9). This phrase is characteristic of the Deuteronomistic editorial framework.

11. **"Shephelah" (שְׁפֵלָה):** V. 33. Retained untranslated as a recognized geographical term for the low hill country between the Judean highlands and the coastal plain. Translating as "lowland" would obscure the technical geographic designation.

12. **Proper name transliterations:** All place names follow standard scholarly transliterations of the Hebrew, maintaining consistency with the Leningrad Codex vocalization. Compound names with "Beth-" (house of), "En-" (spring of), "Kiriath-" (city of), and "Hazar-" (enclosure of) preserve their Hebrew morphological structure.
